MergedExtension.merge error message diverges from Dart: Dart merged_extension.dart line 38 uses left.span.message('') to produce a formatted span message like 'line 5, column 3 of input.scss'. Scala MergedExtension.merge line 163 uses left.span.toString which likely produces a different format. The error string should use the span formatting API to match Dart output.

selector functions missing assertNotBogus validation: Dart extend/replace/unify/is-superselector all call .assertNotBogus(name: 'selector') etc. on parsed selectors (Dart lines 101-151). Port asSelectorList does not call assertNotBogus, allowing bogus selectors to pass through without error.

MultiSpan missing highlightMultiple() (original multi_span.dart:62-75) and messageMultiple() (original multi_span.dart:77-92) which merge primary and additional secondary spans. These are key methods for multi-location error messages. The ported highlight() and message() methods use a simple loop instead of delegating to the source_span package's highlightMultiple/messageMultiple with color support.

MultiSpan.highlight() and message() in original (multi_span.dart:47-60) accept a dynamic color parameter and delegate to _primary.highlightMultiple/messageMultiple with color support including primaryColor/secondaryColor. Port uses no color parameters.

— Reclassified as minor documentation issue. The relocation of compoundIsSuperselector, unifyUniversalAndElement, _findPseudoElementIndexed, _compoundComponentsIsSuperselector, _selectorPseudoIsSuperselector, _selectorPseudoArgs to CompoundSelector.scala and SimpleSelector.scala is an acceptable architectural choice. Only the migration notes header needs updating.

selector.nest() parent selector validation differs: Dart line 55-56 uses assertSelector(allowParent: !first) which validates that the FIRST selector does NOT contain & but subsequent selectors MAY. Port lines 160-168 uses SelectorList.findParentSelector to check after parsing but this approach checks differently than the parser-level allowParent flag.

selector.extend()/replace() use runExtendPipeline which iterates all simples in target compound and adds extensions for each. Dart uses ExtensionStore.extend/replace static methods directly (lines 108-113, 126-131). The internal mechanics may differ since Dart's static methods handle the extension registration internally.

importer/utils.dart is entirely unported. Missing: resolveImportPath() (lines 58-74) with extension/partial/directory resolution logic, _tryPathWithExtensions() (lines 77-79), _tryPath() (lines 86-89), _tryPathAsDirectory() (lines 95-102), _exactlyOne() (lines 108-113), fromImport getter (lines 19-22), canonicalizeContext getter (lines 25-34), inImportRule() (lines 38-48), withCanonicalizeContext() (lines 51-52), isValidUrlScheme() (lines 121-124), _ifInImport() (line 118). These are critical for @import vs @use semantics.

Backward-compat AST-based extension methods (addExtensionAst, extendList, extendComplex, weaveExtension, weaveExtensionNonEmpty, weaveExtensionRest, substituteInComplexUnified, substituteInComplexUnifiedNonEmpty, substituteInComplex, mergeSimples, hasAstExtensions, astTargets) on lines 928-1112 and ExtendUtils.isPlaceholderOnly on lines 1116-1123 are invented code not present in dart-sass. These should be removed or moved to a separate file once EvaluateVisitor is rewired to use the dart addExtension + addSelector API.

CanonicalizeContext port (ImporterResult.scala:40-43) is missing containingUrl access tracking (wasContainingUrlAccessed flag) and containingUrlWithoutMarking getter from original canonicalize_context.dart:17-31. Also missing withFromImport() method (lines 34-43) that temporarily changes fromImport for @import rule processing.

list.append() uses SassList constructor directly instead of Dart's list.withListContents(newList, separator: separator) on line 112. Dart's withListContents preserves the receiver's type (a SassMap would stay a SassMap with list contents). Port line 153 always creates SassList, losing the receiver's original type. However, since brackets are preserved from list.hasBrackets, this may only matter for map inputs.

FilesystemImporter missing couldCanonicalize() method from filesystem.dart:103-115 which checks whether a URL could potentially match a canonical URL based on basename comparison. Also missing modificationTime() method (line 101) and the _loadPathDeprecated/warnForDeprecation logic from filesystem.dart:30-84 which emits fs-importer-cwd deprecation warnings.

Global list includes sqrt/pow/sin/cos/tan/asin/acos/atan/log/clamp/hypot which are NOT in Dart's global list. Dart only has abs/ceil/floor/max/min/percentage/random/round/unit/comparable/unitless as globals. The extra functions in the port's global list will cause them to be resolvable without @use sass:math, which differs from dart-sass behavior.

NodePackageImporter missing package.json exports field resolution from node_package.dart:75-196. Original has full Node resolution algorithm: _resolvePackageExports(), _nodePackageExportsResolve(), _packageTargetResolve(), _getMainExport(), _exportsToCheck(), _compareExpansionKeys(). Port only reads sass/style/main string fields from package.json with a hand-written JSON scanner. Missing: conditional exports (sass/style/default conditions), subpath patterns with wildcard, expansion key comparator, array/map target resolution.

Global abs() deprecation warning text differs from Dart: Dart global abs lines 20-42 emits 'Passing percentage units to the global abs() function is deprecated' for % units, and calls warnForGlobalBuiltIn('math','abs') for non-% units. Port globalAbsFn lines 460-466 emits similar but slightly different text and doesn't call warnForGlobalBuiltIn.

NodePackageImporter missing isNonCanonicalScheme() override from node_package.dart:29 (should return true for scheme=='pkg'). Also missing URL validation from canonicalize() in node_package.dart:36-44 (checks for authority, absolute path, empty path, query/fragment). Also missing _packageNameAndSubpath() proper URL-based splitting (lines 113-122) which handles scoped packages using p.url path operations rather than simple string splitting.

DeprecationProcessing.scala is a thin factory that delegates to DeprecationProcessingLogger in Logger.scala. The actual DeprecationProcessingLogger implementation in Logger.scala is complete with validate(), summarize(), and handleDeprecation(). This is a valid architectural decision. Minor: the logger/DeprecationProcessing.scala file duplicates no logic; verify no callers import from the logger package specifically vs the parent package.

Global list functions missing withDeprecationWarning wrappers: Dart wraps all 9 global list functions with .withDeprecationWarning('list') to emit deprecation when accessed globally. Port registers them directly without warning. Same pattern as all other function files.

initialQuoted() extension (SourceSpan.scala:267-283) scans for quote character from the start but original span.dart:67-76 asserts the span STARTS with a quote character and reads from position 0 directly. Port incorrectly searches for the first quote anywhere in the text and then takes a subspan from that position, not from 0. Should read scanner.readChar() as the quote at position 0 per the original.
Global map functions missing withDeprecationWarning wrappers: Dart wraps all 6 global map functions with .withDeprecationWarning('map'). Port registers them without warning.

withoutInitialAtRule() extension (SourceSpan.scala:254-263) scans for @+identifier+whitespace but does not call trimLeft() on the result like the original span.dart:84 does. Also the ported version uses Character.isWhitespace (Java method) instead of CharCode.isWhitespace for whitespace detection.

Global math functions missing withDeprecationWarning wrappers: Dart wraps ceil/floor/max/min/percentage/random/round/unit with .withDeprecationWarning('math') and comparable/unitless with .withDeprecationWarning('math').withName(). Port registers them without warning.

withoutNamespace() extension (SourceSpan.scala:246-250) searches for a dot and returns everything after it, but original span.dart:62 calls withoutInitialIdentifier().subspan(1) which skips the identifier and the single separator character (the dot). Port would break if the identifier itself contains a dot.
Global meta functions missing withDeprecationWarning wrappers: Dart wraps all shared meta functions with .withDeprecationWarning('meta') for the global list. Port registers them without warning.
Global selector functions missing withDeprecationWarning wrappers: Dart wraps all 8 global selector functions with .withDeprecationWarning('selector'). Port registers them without warning.
Global string functions missing withDeprecationWarning wrappers: Dart wraps all 9 global string functions with .withDeprecationWarning('string'). Port registers them without warning.

CharCode.opposite() (line 205-213) adds reverse mappings (rparen->lparen, rbracket->lbracket, rbrace->lbrace) that are not in the original character.dart:155-162 which only maps opening to closing brackets. These extra reverse mappings are harmless but represent invented logic not in the original.

MapUtil.setAll() helper was added (not in original map.dart) for MergedMapView initialization. This is acceptable utility code. However, map.dart also has pairs extension (redundant in Scala) and getOption extension (also redundant since Scala Map.get returns Option). No gap.

saturate() CSS filter overload handling differs from Dart: Dart has two overloads via overloadedFunction: single-arg () that checks for SassNumber/isSpecialNumber and returns CSS function string, and two-arg (,). Port lines 965-991 uses a single callback with isSingleArg detection and a special null-gap check for named args. The SassNull gap handling is non-standard and the single-arg SassNumber case should return SassString via functionString not via inline string interpolation to match Dart.

Accessors (red/green/blue/hue/saturation/lightness) missing deprecation warnings: Dart _channelFunction (lines 1964-1985) emits Deprecation.colorFunctions warning with suggestion to use color.channel(). Port lines 807-823 return the value directly without any deprecation warning.

Red/green/blue accessors use fuzzyRound on channel values (red255/green255/blue255 at lines 69-76) which rounds to nearest integer. Dart _channelFunction for red/green/blue (lines 1971-1972) returns SassNumber(getter(color), unit) where getter is (color) => color.red, which returns the RAW channel value without rounding. The rounding in the port changes the returned value for non-integer RGB values.

LazyFileSpan missing compareTo() and context delegates from original lazy_file_span.dart:23 and :26. Also missing union() delegate (original line 57). These are part of the FileSpan interface contract in the original Dart source_span package.

mix() legacy path missing isLegacy validation and _checkPercent: Dart _mix (lines 775-808) validates weight with _checkPercent (emitting deprecation for non-% weight), checks color1.isLegacy and color2.isLegacy and throws specific error with color1/color2 parameter names if non-legacy. Port lines 848-895 does not check isLegacy (ISS-278 still open) and doesn't call checkPercent.

mix() weight extraction uses scalar() instead of Dart's weight.valueInRangeWithUnit(0,100,'weight','%') / 100 for the method-based path, and weight.valueInRange(0,100,'weight') / 100 for legacy path. scalar() has no range validation, accepting negative or >100% weights silently.
ISS-565: whitespaceWithoutComments base behavior fixed to consume ALL whitespace including newlines
ISS-572: wrapSpanFormatException only caught StringScannerException. Now catches MultiSpanSassFormatException, SassFormatException, and StringScannerException with span adjustment.
SelectorParser still uses custom text scanner bypassing Parser.scanner. Missing _aNPlusB, nth-child of-selector, proper FileSpan tracking. ISS-546 confirmed open.

AtRootQueryParser still uses custom text scanner bypassing Parser.scanner. parse() uses own fail() instead of wrapSpanFormatException, never calls scanner.expectDone(), readIdentifier() lacks escape handling. ISS-542 confirmed still open.

Expression parser still uses text-based _parseSimpleExpression for _expression() codepath alongside partial recursive-descent _rdExpression. dart-sass has a single unified _expression/_singleExpression. Two parallel parsers remain: _expression collects raw text then delegates to _parseSimpleExpression which does regex-like splitting, while _rdExpression is the faithful port. ISS-329/ISS-531 confirmed still open.

StylesheetParser missing dart-sass methods: (1) _functionChild (stylesheet.dart:755-795) - validates that only variable declarations and control flow are allowed inside @function, (2) _declarationAtRule (lines 737-752) - limits at-rules inside declarations, (3) _plainAtRuleName (lines 798-802) - consumes at-rule name without interpolation, (4) _disallowedAtRule (lines 1797-1801) - throws error for forbidden at-rules. Currently _atRule handles all dispatch but lacks these context-specific restrictions.

StylesheetParser._atRule uses plain identifier() for the at-rule name instead of interpolatedIdentifier(). dart-sass stylesheet.dart line 675 uses interpolatedIdentifier() so that @#{foo}-rule with interpolation in the name is supported. ISS-400 confirmed still open.

StylesheetParser._atRule case 'import' does not emit Deprecation.import warning for DynamicImport arguments. dart-sass stylesheet.dart lines 1190-1200 add a warning for every DynamicImport. Also missing: rejection of dynamic imports inside control directives or mixins (line 1201-1203).
rgb/rgba 2-arg path lines 537-548 still missing Dart rgbTwoArg logic: no isSpecialVariable checks, no isLegacy throw, no toSpace(rgb), no isSpecialNumber on 2nd arg. See Dart 1388-1423.

StylesheetParser._atRule case 'mixin' does not validate --prefix names (css-function-mixin deprecation) per dart-sass stylesheet.dart lines 1462-1469, does not check _inMixin/_inContentBlock nesting (lines 1477-1487), and does not check _inControlDirective (lines 1482-1487). ISS-388 confirmed still open.

StylesheetParser._atRule case 'extend' does not validate context (_inStyleRule/_inMixin/_inContentBlock) per dart-sass stylesheet.dart lines 931-936. Also uses raw text collection instead of almostAnyValue() for the selector (lines 938-939), and does not call expectStatementSeparator after the rule.

StylesheetParser missing mozDocumentRule faithful port. dart-sass stylesheet.dart lines 1504-1588 has specialized parsing for url/url-prefix/domain/regexp functions with deprecation warnings. The port case-matches '-moz-document' in the catch-all at-rule handler which only emits a deprecation warning and collects raw text.
StylesheetParser._atRule case 'content' does not validate _inMixin context per dart-sass stylesheet.dart lines 855-861. Port accepts @content outside mixin declarations.

StylesheetParser._atRule case 'function' missing _inMixin/_inContentBlock and _inControlDirective checks per dart-sass stylesheet.dart lines 987-997. Also missing function-name deprecation warning for case-insensitive reserved names (lines 970-981).

StylesheetParser._atRule catch-all (unknownAtRule) collects raw text instead of using _interpolatedDeclarationValue. dart-sass unknownAtRule (lines 1759-1790) uses _interpolatedDeclarationValue(allowOpenBrace: false), sets _inUnknownAtRule and _inPlainCssFunction flags, and restores them in a finally block. The port sets _inUnknownAtRule but does not use _interpolatedDeclarationValue, does not set _inPlainCssFunction, and has no finally-block restoration.

StylesheetParser._topLevelStatement does not handle indented-syntax shortcuts: dart-sass stylesheet.dart lines 201-214 handle '+' at start of line as @include (indented syntax) and '=' at start as @mixin. These are missing from the port's dispatch.

StylesheetParser._topLevelStatement does not dispatch '}' to error('unmatched "}"') per dart-sass stylesheet.dart line 217. Also does not call _variableDeclarationOrStyleRule (lines 220-222) which handles namespace-qualified variable declarations at the top level.

CssParser uses post-parse AST-walk to reject Sass syntax instead of dart-sass inline parse-time rejection. This means error source spans point at AST node spans rather than the exact scanner position where rejection should occur. Also missing: identifierLike override that rejects _disallowedFunctionNames at parse time, parentheses override that enforces expression-only-in-calculations, _cssFunctionRule for custom-ident @function, _cssImportRule with interpolation-free URL checking. ISS-520/ISS-330 partially addressed but architecture gap remains.

SassParser._importArgument is private and annotated @nowarn('unused private member'). It will not be called until importArgument is extracted as a virtual method in StylesheetParser. dart-sass sass.dart importArgument() override is a public method override. This needs to be wired into the import parsing path. ISS-519 partially addressed but importArgument wiring remains.

ScssParser.styleRuleSelector uses raw text collection with manual brace/bracket/quote balancing instead of dart-sass almostAnyValue(). dart-sass scss.dart line 20: 'styleRuleSelector() => almostAnyValue()'. The port is 65 lines vs 1 line. While functionally similar, it duplicates logic that exists in the base class almostAnyValue method.

StylesheetParser._atRule case 'for' uses lookahead workaround (_lookingAtToOrThrough) for from-expression termination. dart-sass stylesheet.dart lines 1028-1042 uses _expression(until: ...) with inline scanIdentifier('to'/'through') that sets exclusive flag. The port checks identifier text via peek but does not set exclusive inside the until callback — the exclusive flag is set afterward, potentially allowing the from-expression to consume the to/through keyword.

StylesheetGraph is a minimal stub vs original. Missing modifiedSince, reload, reloadAllModified, remove, _add, _nodeFor, _upstreamNodes, _recanonicalizeImports, _ignoreErrors. StylesheetNode missing upstream/upstreamImports maps, downstream set, _replaceUpstream, _remove.

compile() throws UnsupportedOperationException instead of real logic. Original compile.dart:40-105 uses DeprecationProcessingLogger, checks syntax vs path to decide importCache.importCanonical vs Stylesheet.parse, passes FilesystemImporter.cwd. Also missing: compileString relative-URL deprecation warning (lines 145-153), _compileStylesheet useSpaces/indentWidth/lineFeed params, source-map URL rewriting (lines 223-237).

BuiltInCallable._overloads list not ported. Original built_in.dart stores List of (ParameterList, Callback) overloads. Port uses a single callback+signature. callbackFor(positional, names) method (built_in.dart:108-134) with fuzzy matching logic not ported - port uses BuiltInOverloadDispatch as workaround but does not match the original overload resolution algorithm (mismatch distance, parameter count comparison).

BuiltInCallable.mixin factory does not wrap callback to return sassNull. Original built_in.dart:53-67 mixin constructor wraps the void callback to return sassNull. Port BuiltInCallable.mixin passes callback directly without wrapping.

BuiltInCallable.function and .mixin do not parse ParameterList from signature string. Original built_in.dart:34-43 calls ParameterList.parse to create a real AST node. Port stores raw string in signature field and sets parameters to Nullable.empty. This means the evaluator cannot perform standard arity checking against the parsed parameter list.

BuiltInCallable.overloadedFunction does not parse ParameterList from each overload signature. Original built_in.dart:87-98 calls ParameterList.parse for each overload. Port does manual string splitting instead of using the real parser, losing default value validation and rest-parameter semantics.

ImportCache._toImporters is a no-op that just returns importers without processing loadPaths. Original import_cache.dart:119-135 converts loadPaths to FilesystemImporter instances and reads SASS_PATH environment variable. Port comment says callers handle it but the constructor accepting loadPaths silently drops them.

Configuration.throwErrorForUnknownVariables creates a synthetic empty span instead of using the actual configuration span. Original does not have this method - the error is thrown by the evaluator which has access to the real nodeWithSpan. The port creates a fake SourceFile and FileSpan which will produce unhelpful error locations.

Module.setVariable signature takes (name, value) but original module.dart:75 takes (name, value, nodeWithSpan). The nodeWithSpan parameter is needed for source-span tracking of variable writes through forwarded modules. Same issue in ForwardedView.setVariable and ShadowedView.setVariable.

rgb/rgba overloaded function structure: Dart uses BuiltInCallable.overloadedFunction with 4 distinct named-param signatures (red/green/blue/alpha, red/green/blue, color/alpha, channels). Port uses single function with max-args dispatching by args.length. This means named parameters like rgb(dollar-channels: ...) or rgb(dollar-color: red, dollar-alpha: 0.5) cannot be resolved by name. Dart lines 53-75.

hsl/hsla overloaded function structure: Dart uses BuiltInCallable.overloadedFunction with 4 signatures including 2-arg (hue,saturation) that checks isSpecialVariable. Port uses single function dispatching by args.length. This also means hsl(dollar-channels: ...) named param dispatch is lost. Dart lines 107-145.

mix() legacy path missing isLegacy validation: Dart lines 792-806 check c1.isLegacy and c2.isLegacy separately with specific error messages naming the color1/color2 parameter. Also missing _checkPercent(weight, weight) call for deprecation warning on non-percent weight. Port lines 879-898 proceed unconditionally.

Environment.toImplicitConfiguration returns Map[String, Value] instead of Configuration. Original environment.dart:843-861 returns Configuration.implicit with ConfiguredValue entries that include both the value AND the variableNode. Port loses the variable nodes, meaning source-span attribution for configured values is lost.

Environment.EnvironmentModule.transitivelyContainsCss does not check preModuleComments.isNotEmpty. Original _EnvironmentModule (environment.dart:1019-1022) includes preModuleComments.isNotEmpty in the transitivelyContainsCss check. Port only checks css.children.nonEmpty and allModules.

to-gamut() still silently defaults to localMinde when method is null. Dart lines 666-673 throw SassScriptException requiring method arg with specific message. Port line 1761 defaults to localMinde without error. ISS-534 was resolved but the code still has this gap.

to-gamut() missing space.isBounded check: Dart line 681 returns color unchanged if space is not bounded. Port line 1763 proceeds to gamut mapping regardless. Also Dart validates method via GamutMapMethod.fromName BEFORE checking isBounded so invalid names always error.

Environment.EnvironmentModule.cloneCss uses a shallow CssStylesheet copy instead of the original cloneCssStylesheet function. Original environment.dart:1129-1149 calls cloneCssStylesheet(css, extensionStore) which produces a deep clone with a fresh ExtensionStore that mirrors the extension state. Port does CssStylesheet(css.children.toList, css.span) which is a shallow copy that may share mutable child nodes.

Environment.addModule error for duplicate namespace uses SassScriptException instead of MultiSpanSassScriptException. Original environment.dart:267-270 throws MultiSpanSassScriptException with the original @use span as a secondary span. Port throws plain SassScriptException losing the secondary span context.

invert() legacy no-space path (lines 1130-1134) uses scalar(weightNumber, 100)/100 instead of Dart _checkPercent(weightNumber, weight) for deprecation + validation, then uses custom mixColors instead of Dart _mixLegacy. Also uses invertChannel with only 3 channels (r,g,b) instead of Dart destructuring channels list. Dart lines 880-901.

Environment._assertNoConflicts throws plain SassScriptException instead of MultiSpanSassScriptException. Original environment.dart:344-351 throws MultiSpanSassScriptException with the original @forward span as secondary. Port loses the secondary span.

Environment._fromOneModule throws plain SassScriptException for ambiguous modules instead of MultiSpanSassScriptException. Original environment.dart:941-951 throws MultiSpanSassScriptException with spans from all conflicting modules. Port loses all secondary spans.

invert() space-aware path (lines 1136-1147) parses spaceArg via InterpolationMethod.fromValue instead of Dart ColorSpace.fromName. Dart line 904-907 expects a plain color space name string with assertString/assertUnquoted. Port allows interpolation method descriptors. Also Dart constructs InterpolationMethod(space) for the interpolate call, not the parsed method.

lighten/darken/saturate/desaturate amount validation: Dart uses amount.valueInRange(0, 100, amount) which throws if amount is outside 0-100. Port uses scalar() which has no range check, silently accepting out-of-range values. Also Dart uses clampLikeCss for the result while port uses hslFrom which uses math.max/min clamping.

Environment.importForwards shadow check omits isEmpty guard. Original environment.dart:397-399 checks if(!shadowed.isEmpty) before adding the shadowed module back. Port (lines 364-366) always adds the shadowed view back via Nullable wrapping without checking if the shadowed module is now empty (has no remaining members).

red/green/blue accessors use math.round on channel values (red255/green255/blue255 at lines 73-80). Dart _channelFunction returns raw getter value without rounding. ISS-672 was resolved but the rounding is still present in the code.

parseSignature is in Callable.scala but the original utils.dart:478-494 catches SassFormatException and re-throws with a more descriptive message. Both versions exist but Utils.scala is missing: throwWithTrace, attachTrace, getTrace (trace propagation utilities, utils.dart:448-470), mapAsync, putIfAbsentAsync (async helpers, utils.dart:383-402), and castOrNull (utils.dart:161). The trace utilities are important for proper error chaining.

keywords() still returns empty map for non-SassArgumentList instead of throwing. Dart meta.dart line 81 throws dollar-args: X is not an argument list. Port line 193-195 returns SassMap.empty silently. ISS-632 still open and valid.

ImporterUtils missing resolveImportPath and related filesystem helpers. Original utils.dart:58-118 has resolveImportPath, _tryPathWithExtensions, _tryPath, _tryPathAsDirectory, _exactlyOne. These are used by FilesystemImporter. The JVM-only ImporterFileUtils may have them but ImporterUtils.scala should declare the interface or cross-platform stubs.

module() = global ::: moduleOnly includes runtime-context functions (variable-exists, function-exists etc.) in both global AND module lists. Dart only puts feature-exists/inspect/type-of/keywords in the shared _shared list. Runtime functions should only be in the module, not the global list.

ImporterResult.sourceMapUrl defaults to Nullable.empty when not provided. Original result.dart:26-27 generates a data: URI from contents+utf8 when sourceMapUrl is null. This means source maps will be missing URL references for stylesheets loaded without explicit sourceMapUrl.
ImporterResult constructor does not validate sourceMapUrl is absolute. Original result.dart:48-49 throws ArgumentError.value if sourceMapUrl.scheme is empty (relative). Port accepts any string.

global list includes ifFn WITHOUT withDeprecationWarning which is correct (Dart does not wrap if). But the global list also includes many runtime functions (variable-exists, function-exists, mixin-exists, global-variable-exists, content-exists, module-variables, module-functions, module-mixins, get-function, get-mixin, call) with .withDeprecationWarning(meta). In Dart these runtime functions are defined in the evaluator, not in the shared globals.

Logger.warnForDeprecation skips future deprecations unconditionally. Original logger.dart:106-107 only skips future deprecations when the logger is NOT a DeprecationProcessingLogger. Port Logger trait method (line 44) skips all future deprecations. The DeprecationProcessingLogger overrides warnForDeprecation correctly but the base trait method is wrong for loggers that ARE DeprecationProcessingLogger calling super.

extend/replace/unify/is-superselector still missing assertNotBogus validation on parsed selectors. Dart lines 101-151 call .assertNotBogus(name: selector) etc. on all three selector arguments. Port asSelectorList does not validate bogus selectors. ISS-643 still valid.

EvaluationContext trait uses currentCallableNode (returning AstNode) but original evaluation_context.dart:49 only exposes currentCallableSpan (returning FileSpan). The currentCallableNode field is an SSG addition not in the original. Also EvaluationContext.current returns Nullable instead of throwing StateError when no context is active, which differs from the original static getter (evaluation_context.dart:21-23).

nest() uses SelectorList.findParentSelector post-parse instead of Dart assertSelector(allowParent: !first) at parse time. The Dart approach catches parent selectors during parsing with proper error context. Port approach checks after parsing which may produce different error messages.

Global alpha() overloaded function: Dart has BuiltInCallable.overloadedFunction(alpha) with two signatures: single-arg dollar-color (with Microsoft filter regex check, isLegacy check) and dollar-args... (multi-arg Microsoft filter check). Port alphaFn line 829-839 only handles single arg with simple s.text.contains(=) check instead of _microsoftFilterStart regex. Missing the multi-arg overload entirely and the SassColor(isLegacy: false) throw.

Module-scope alpha() in Dart (lines 551-598) has overloadedFunction with Microsoft filter deprecation (Deprecation.colorModuleCompat) and different error messages than global. Port moduleAlphaFn line 1920-1921 is a simple assertColor().alpha wrapper with no Microsoft filter handling or deprecation.

Module-scope _removedColorFunction entries not ported: Dart module lines 488-549 registers 9 error-throwing stubs for adjust-hue/lighten/darken/saturate/desaturate/opacify/fade-in/transparentize/fade-out that throw function X() isnt in sass:color module with recommendation. Port module list lines 1949-1953 filters override names but does not include these error stubs.

Module-scope invert() differs: Dart module invert lines 460-472 wraps the result of _invert and checks if result is SassString to emit Deprecation.colorModuleCompat warning for number arguments. Port shares the global invertFn for module scope without this SassString check and colorModuleCompat warning.
Module-scope opacity() in Dart lines 601-616 checks for SassNumber arg and emits Deprecation.colorModuleCompat with recommendation. Port moduleOpacityFn line 1918 only does assertColor().alpha.
Module-scope grayscale() in Dart lines 494-508 checks for SassNumber arg and emits Deprecation.colorModuleCompat with recommendation. Port moduleGrayscaleFn lines 1903-1915 only handles color path.
SerializeVisitor visitCssComment missing multi-line comment re-indentation: Dart visitCssComment calls _minimumIndentation and _writeWithIndent. Scala appends node.text verbatim. Re-audit of ISS-612.

adjust-hue() missing _angleValue call for degrees: Dart line 160 uses _angleValue(arguments[1], degrees) which coerces angle units (deg/rad/grad/turn). Port line 1030 uses args(1).assertNumber().value which ignores units. Also missing deprecation warning with suggestedValue.

opacify/transparentize amount validation: Dart uses amount.valueInRangeWithUnit(0, 1, amount, empty-string) which validates 0-1 range. Port line 1230/1248 uses scalar() with no range check. Also Dart adds clampLikeCss for the final alpha; port uses simple clamp.

SerializeVisitor visitCssImport missing compressed-mode URL optimization: Dart _writeImportUrl (lines 277-294) strips url() wrapper in compressed mode and adds quotes if needed. Scala visitCssImport writes node.url.value directly without optimization. Re-audit of ISS-615.

SerializeVisitor missing _tryPrivateUseCharacter: Dart (lines 1475-1511) escapes Private Use Area characters in expanded mode for both quoted and unquoted strings. Scala formatString does not handle PUA characters at all. Glyph font stylesheets may output raw PUA chars instead of hex escapes.

SerializeVisitor _visitUnquotedString simplified: Dart (lines 1452-1473) collapses newline+following-spaces to single space AND skips spaces after newlines (afterNewline flag). Scala formatString for unquoted (line 822-833) only replaces newlines with spaces but does NOT collapse post-newline whitespace. Output may contain extra spaces.

divFn uses EvaluationContext.warnForDeprecation(Deprecation.SlashDiv, ...) but Dart line 264 uses plain warn() not warnForDeprecation. This causes the warning to be emitted as a deprecation rather than a simple warning.

SerializeVisitor visitCssSupportsRule missing compressed-mode space optimization: Dart (lines 340-355) omits space after @supports when condition starts with '(' in compressed mode. Scala (line 1619-1624) always writes '@supports ' with a trailing space regardless of mode.

SerializeVisitor missing _visitQuotedString forceDoubleQuote restart: Dart (lines 1360-1449) has sophisticated quote-selection that restarts serialization with forceDoubleQuote=true when it encounters a double-quote after already recording single-quotes. Scala formatString (line 822-870) does a pre-scan to choose quote character but does not handle the interleaved restart case where both quote types appear. Rare edge case affecting strings containing both quote characters.

SerializeVisitor visitCssDeclaration missing custom property error handling: Dart (lines 357-399) wraps value serialization in try/catch for MultiSpanSassScriptException and SassScriptException, converting them to SassExceptions with proper span info. Also handles _writeFoldedValue/_writeReindentedValue distinction via parsedAsSassScript flag AND uses _for(node.value) for source-map span tracking. Scala (line 1356-1391) has the parsedAsSassScript branching and custom property handling but lacks the try/catch error conversion and _for source-map span wrapping.

Global math list still includes sqrt/sin/cos/tan/asin/acos/atan/log/pow/clamp/hypot which are NOT in Dart global list. These should only be available under sass:math module. ISS-654 was resolved but the code still has them at lines 495-496.

EvaluateVisitor infrastructure methods are ported but annotated @nowarn unused: _withStackFrame (line 1865), _withoutSlash (line 1881), _warnWithSpan (line 1931), _addExceptionSpan (line 1993), _addExceptionTrace (line 2016), _addErrorSpan (line 2033), _expressionNode (line 2051) all carry @nowarn unused annotations indicating they are not yet wired into all call sites that need them. The methods themselves are faithfully ported but many callers still use direct exception throwing instead of _addExceptionSpan wrapping. ISS-592 should be updated to track wiring rather than porting.

EvaluateVisitor visitInterpolatedFunctionExpression simplified: Dart (lines 3068-3084) evaluates interpolated function names, then checks if the result matches a calculation function name and dispatches to _visitCalculation before falling through to plain CSS. Scala (line 1258-1263) always renders as plain CSS without calculation dispatch. Interpolated function calls like ${calc}(1 + 2) will output calc(1 + 2) literally instead of evaluating to 3.

Missing AnySelectorVisitor: Dart any_selector.dart (38 LOC) defines AnySelectorVisitor mixin that returns true if ANY selector node matches via .any() recursion. No Scala counterpart exists. Used internally by dart-sass for selector predicate checks. Re-confirms ISS-606.

Module composition at line 1949-1953 derives from global list by filtering overrides. Dart module (lines 451-771) is a completely separate list with distinct function instances. This means module functions inherit global withDeprecationWarning wrappers and then get filtered, which may not match Dart where module functions are unwrapped.

whiteness/blackness module functions missing _channelFunction deprecation pattern: Dart module uses _channelFunction(whiteness/blackness, ColorSpace.hwb, ..., unit: percent) which emits Deprecation.colorFunctions warning with suggestion to use color.channel(). Port whitenessFn/blacknessFn lines 1865-1876 are simple accessors without deprecation.

visitStylesheet missing globalVariables pass: Dart (line 1177-1183) iterates node.globalVariables.pairs after visiting children, creating guarded VariableDeclaration for each. Scala (line 2511-2518) only visits children and misses this pass.

visitBinaryOperationExpression missing plain CSS operator check: Dart (line 2769-2776) checks _stylesheet.plainCss and throws 'Operators are not allowed in plain CSS' for operators other than singleEquals and dividedBy. Scala (line 1214-1235) lacks this check entirely, allowing all operators in plain CSS mode.

visitParenthesizedExpression missing plain CSS check: Dart (line 3001-3006) throws 'Parentheses are not allowed in plain CSS' when _stylesheet.plainCss is true. Scala (line 1497-1498) unconditionally evaluates the inner expression without any plain CSS check.

visitForRule missing @return/early-exit handling: Dart visitForRule (line 1631-1675) uses _handleReturn to short-circuit the loop when @return is executed in the body. Scala visitForRule (line 2893-2947) runs the body statements without catching ReturnSignal from @return, so a @return inside a @for will crash instead of cleanly exiting.

visitEachRule missing @return/early-exit handling: Dart visitEachRule (line 1432-1453) uses _handleReturn to short-circuit the loop. Scala visitEachRule (line 2949-2974) runs body without catching ReturnSignal, so @return inside @each will crash.

visitWhileRule missing @return/early-exit handling: Dart visitWhileRule (line 2747-2764) uses _handleReturn to break from the loop on @return. Scala visitWhileRule (line 2976-2990) has no return-catching mechanism; @return inside @while will crash. Also adds artificial 100000-iteration guard not present in Dart.

ARCH: _statement dispatch split into _topLevelStatement+_childStatement instead of dart-sass single _statement. Top-level routes dollar to _variableDeclaration directly, skipping _variableDeclarationOrStyleRule. Default case goes to _styleRule, missing _variableDeclarationOrStyleRule path.

visitIfRule missing @return/early-exit propagation: Dart visitIfRule (line 1825-1844) uses _handleReturn so @return inside @if body propagates correctly. Scala visitIfRule (line 2870-2891) just evaluates children without propagating ReturnSignal, so @return inside @if inside a @function will crash.

_slash missing recommendation with expressionToCalc: Dart _slash (line 2827-2854) generates both math.div() and expressionToCalc() recommendations in the slash-div deprecation warning. Scala _slash (line 177-196) only shows a generic message without the AST-based recommendation or expressionToCalc() call.

MISSING: Entire selector subsystem from dart-sass lines 3908-4310 not ported in StylesheetParser. Missing methods: _selectorList, _complexSelector, _compoundSelector, _simpleSelector, _attributeSelector, _classSelector, _idSelector, _placeholderSelector, _pseudoSelector, _parentSelector, _typeOrUniversalSelector, _isSimpleSelectorStart (12 methods, ~400 LOC). These are used when _parseSelectors=true.

visitMapExpression missing multi-span error for duplicate keys: Dart (line 3018-3038) uses MultiSpanSassRuntimeException with both first-key and second-key spans. Scala (line 1476-1489) throws a plain SassScriptException without secondary span information.

visitFunctionExpression missing _inFunction flag: Dart (line 3092-3098) sets _inFunction=true before running function callable and restores it after. Scala visitFunctionExpression (line 1242-1380) never sets _inFunction, so content like loud comments inside function calls may be incorrectly emitted to CSS output.

MISSING: Entire media query subsystem from dart-sass lines 4315-4502 not ported. Missing methods: _mediaQueryList, _mediaQuery, _mediaLogicSequence, _mediaOrInterp, _mediaInParens, _expressionUntilComparison (6 methods, ~190 LOC). Port uses ad-hoc StringBuilder-based raw text collection for media queries instead of the structured parser.

MISSING: mozDocumentRule not ported (dart-sass lines 1504-1588, 85 LOC). Port just emits a deprecation warning and falls through to generic unknownAtRule. Dart-sass has specialized parsing for url/url-prefix/domain/regexp functions with specific syntax rules and a needsDeprecationWarning flag.

visitVariableDeclaration missing new-global deprecation warning: Dart (line 2673-2690) checks _environment.globalVariableExists and emits Deprecation.newGlobal warning when a !global assignment declares a new variable. Scala visitVariableDeclaration (line 2825-2868) has no new-global deprecation check.

visitWarnRule simplified output: Dart (line 2735-2745) calls _serialize to properly serialize the value with span context. Scala visitWarnRule (line 3003-3011) uses toCssString(quote=false) and does not pass the value through _serialize with span-based exception wrapping.

visitErrorRule simplified output: Dart (line 1476-1481) calls error.toString() on the value and throws _exception with node.span. Scala visitErrorRule (line 3014-3021) also calls toCssString(quote=false) instead of toString, potentially producing different error messages (e.g. for maps, lists).

MISSING: _importRule not faithfully ported (dart-sass lines 1185-1211). Port inlines import parsing in _atRule case 'import' (lines 271-325) with ad-hoc raw text collection instead of calling importArgument(). Missing: DynamicImport deprecation warning (Deprecation.import), parseImportUrl URI validation with Windows path backwards-compat, isPlainImportUrl method, proper importArgument dispatch.

MISSING: _importSupportsQuery and _tryImportSupportsFunction not ported (dart-sass lines 1338-1385, 48 LOC). These parse supports() conditions inside @import modifiers. Port _tryImportModifiers uses raw text collection instead.

MISSING: _addOrInject helper not ported (dart-sass line 4816-4822). Used by _atRootQuery to inject unquoted string expressions into InterpolationBuffer. Currently _atRootQuery is also not faithfully ported.

visitExtendRule missing bogus-combinator deprecation warning: Dart (line 1492-1506) iterates styleRule.originalSelector.components and emits Deprecation.bogusCombinators for bogus/useless selectors used as extenders. Scala visitExtendRule (line 4165-4212) does not emit these warnings.

visitStyleRule text-based selector expansion diverges from Dart AST approach: Dart (line 2372-2483) uses SelectorList.parse + nestWithin on AST objects with full validation of plainCss leading combinators, merge logic based on fromPlainCss, and containsParentSelector checks. Scala visitStyleRule (line 2520-2670) uses a text-based _expandSelector for parent resolution and wraps the result in _syntheticSelectorList as fallback, which bypasses structural CSS validation.

visitDeclaration missing _styleRule/context check: Dart (line 1372-1378) checks _styleRule==null && !_inUnknownAtRule && !_inKeyframes and throws 'Declarations may only be used within style rules'. Scala visitDeclaration (line 2742-2818) does not perform this check, allowing declarations outside style rules.

visitLegacyIfExpression missing _evaluateMacroArguments and _verifyArguments: Dart (line 2981-2993) uses _evaluateMacroArguments + _verifyArguments against LegacyIfExpression.declaration for proper argument validation. Scala (line 1456-1467) directly accesses positional arguments without verifying count or handling named arguments (condition, if-true, if-false).

ARCH: @at-root rule uses ad-hoc StringBuilder text collection (lines 738-801) instead of dart-sass structured parsing. Dart-sass _atRootRule (lines 804-826) has 3 branches: (1) paren query via _atRootQuery, (2) lookingAtChildren for bare block, (3) inline _styleRule child. Port uses raw text scanning with manual depth tracking, then post-hoc checks if text starts with paren.

ARCH: @media rule uses ad-hoc StringBuilder text collection (lines 511-597) instead of dart-sass structured _mediaQueryList parser. Dart-sass mediaRule (lines 1442-1450) calls _mediaQueryList which builds structured Interpolation via _mediaQuery/_mediaInParens/_mediaLogicSequence. Port collects raw text with manual depth/quote tracking, then parses interpolation from the string.

visitAtRule missing _declarationName check: Dart (line 1555-1560) throws 'At-rules may not be used within nested declarations' when _declarationName is set. Scala visitAtRule (line 3048-3131) does not perform this check.

ARCH: @keyframes uses ad-hoc text collection (lines 608-705) instead of dart-sass approach. Dart-sass has no special @keyframes handler - it falls through to unknownAtRule which correctly uses _interpolatedDeclarationValue and _withChildren. Port has ~100 lines of manual scanning code that should be replaced with unknownAtRule dispatch.

visitMediaRule missing _declarationName check: Dart (line 2261-2265) throws 'Media rules may not be used within nested declarations' when _declarationName is set. Scala visitMediaRule (line 3148-3241) does not perform this check.

visitSupportsRule missing _declarationName check: Dart (line 2541-2545) throws 'Supports rules may not be used within nested declarations' when _declarationName is set. Scala visitSupportsRule (line 3623-3675) does not perform this check.

visitDebugRule extra side effect: Dart visitDebugRule (line 1363-1370) calls _logger.debug and returns null. Scala (line 2992-3001) also appends to _warnings buffer with 'DEBUG: ...' prefix. Dart does NOT add debug messages to warnings. This creates spurious entries in CompileResult.warnings.

visitWarnRule missing stack trace: Dart visitWarnRule (line 2741) passes trace: _stackTrace(node.span) to _logger.warn. Scala (line 3003-3011) does not pass any stack trace, so warn output lacks context.

run() missing SassException catch with loadedUrls: Dart run() (line 712-731) wraps the entire evaluation in try-catch and rethrows SassException with error.withLoadedUrls(_loadedUrls). Scala run() (line 531-574) does not catch SassException to attach loaded URLs, so errors will not carry the loaded URL set.

ARCH: unknownAtRule (port _unknownAtRule lines 2276-2331) does not set _inPlainCssFunction flag. Dart-sass unknownAtRule (lines 1759-1790) checks if name equals 'function' case-insensitively and sets _inPlainCssFunction=true, which affects declaration parsing inside the block (custom property result handling). Port never sets this flag.

MISSING: _urlString not ported (dart-sass lines 4781-4793). Port inlines URL string parsing in _useRule and _forwardRule using raw string() calls without URI.parse validation or FormatException error wrapping.

run() missing _addExceptionTrace wrapper: Dart run() (line 724) wraps _execute in _addExceptionTrace so SassExceptions that are not SassRuntimeExceptions get proper stack traces. Scala run() (line 565) calls _execute directly without this wrapper.

MISSING: _useNamespace not faithfully ported (dart-sass lines 1641-1663). Port inlines namespace derivation in _useRule (lines 1159-1176) but does not call Parser.parseIdentifier to validate the namespace, does not handle url.pathSegments properly (uses string splitting instead of URI path segments), and does not throw the helpful error about adding an as clause.

_warnForBogusCombinators missing plainCss check on leadingCombinators branch: Dart (line 2501-2511) only warns for leadingCombinators if !_stylesheet.plainCss. Scala (line 2476-2484) always warns regardless of plainCss, producing spurious warnings for plain CSS files.

_warnForBogusCombinators missing MultiSpan and child-content detail: Dart (line 2513-2535) third branch uses MultiSpan with 'invalid selector' primary and child.span secondary with context-dependent text including comment conversion hint. Scala (line 2486-2498) emits a simpler warning without any secondary span or child-content analysis.

DROPPED: _rdExpression missing bracketList parameter. Dart-sass _expression has bracketList parameter (line 1972) that enables parsing [a, b c] bracket list syntax from within the main expression loop. Port handles bracket lists via separate _rdBracketList method called from _rdSingleExpression, but the main _rdExpression loop at line 3730 delegates to _rdBracketList which internally calls _rdExpression without the bracket-specific whitespace and finalization logic from dart-sass lines 1979-2401.

visitAtRootRule diverges from Dart algorithm: Dart (line 1186-1242) uses _performInterpolationWithMap + AtRootQuery.parse, _trimIncluded for contiguous parent matching, and _scopeForAtRoot for cascading scope adjustments. Scala (line 3677-3787) uses simpler ancestor scanning with _topLevelTokens-based query parsing. Missing: _trimIncluded algorithm, _scopeForAtRoot with layered scope callbacks for media/keyframes/unknownAtRule.

DROPPED: _rdExpression missing strict-unary deprecation warning. Dart-sass lines 2069-2097 emit Deprecation.strictUnary when plus/minus binary ops have ambiguous whitespace (e.g. 1 -2 could be 1 - 2 or 1 (-2)). Port resolveOneOperation does not check whitespace around operators for this warning.

DROPPED: _rdExpression missing plainCss operator restriction. Dart-sass addOperator (lines 2136-2149) checks if plainCss is true and errors on operators other than singleEquals/plus/minus/times/dividedBy. Port addOperator (line 3649) has no plainCss check.

DROPPED: _rdExpression missing _inParentheses restoration on final result. Dart-sass line 2370 restores _inParentheses=wasInParentheses before returning comma expressions. Port line 3899 restores _inExpression but does NOT restore _inParentheses for the comma-expression path.

SassList.toCssString incorrectly filters blank elements for ALL separators. Dart serialize.dart only omits blank elements for non-comma-separated lists when not in inspect mode. Port applies filterNot(_.isBlank) unconditionally which may drop null comma-list elements.

visitVariableExpression missing _addExceptionSpan wrapper: Dart (line 2874-2881) wraps _environment.getVariable in _addExceptionSpan(node, ...) so SassScriptExceptions get proper span context. Scala visitVariableExpression (line 1556-1569) directly calls _environment.getVariable without _addExceptionSpan wrapping.

DROPPED: _rdSelector missing && double-ampersand warning. Dart-sass _selector (lines 2831-2853) detects && (double ampersand) and warns 'you probably want to use and instead'. Port _rdSelector (lines 3948-3954) just reads single & and returns immediately.
DROPPED: _rdVariable missing plainCss check. Dart-sass _variable (lines 2816-2828) errors on variables in plain CSS mode. Port _rdVariable (lines 4191-4196) does not check plainCss.

SerializeVisitor missing constructor parameters quote, useSpaces, indentWidth, lineFeed, logger from Dart lines 153-171. Scala line 45-49 only has style, inspect, sourceMap. Missing quote breaks serializeValue with quote:false. Missing useSpaces/indentWidth hardcodes 2-space indent. Missing lineFeed hardcodes LF.

visitUnaryOperationExpression missing _addExceptionSpan wrapper: Dart (line 2887-2895) wraps the unary operation dispatch in _addExceptionSpan. Scala (line 1542-1552) catches SassScriptException manually but does not use _addExceptionSpan, so the error wrapping differs.

visitStringExpression serialization difference: Dart (line 3975-3996) uses _serialize(result, value, quote: false) for non-SassString interpolation values, which wraps toCssString in _addExceptionSpan. Scala (line 1511-1535) uses other.toCssString(quote = false) directly without span-based exception wrapping.

SassNumber.modulo return type is Value in port (line 401) but SassNumber in Dart (line 754). Dart explicitly returns SassNumber via withValue. Port returns Value due to override signature matching Value.modulo. This may cause issues with downstream code expecting SassNumber.

_performInterpolation missing warnForColor check: Dart _performInterpolation (line 4373-4383) accepts a warnForColor parameter and delegates to _performInterpolationHelper which emits color-in-interpolation warnings. Scala _performInterpolation (line 1919-1932) does not accept or check warnForColor.

DROPPED: _rdUnaryOperation missing plainCss check. Dart-sass _unaryOperation (lines 2653-2669) checks plainCss and errors on operators other than divide. Port _rdUnaryOperation (lines 5119-5131) has no plainCss check.
SassNumber missing deprecated valueInUnits method. Dart number.dart:592-598 has a deprecated alias valueInUnits that delegates to coerceValue. While deprecated, old user code may still call it.

SassNumber.unaryMinus not ported: Dart number.dart does not override unaryMinus (inherits from Value which returns SassString). But SassNumber should return a negated number. Verify that the evaluator handles this correctly via SassNumber(-1).times(this) or similar.

DROPPED: _rdNumber missing allowTrailingDot logic. Dart-sass _number (lines 2681-2712) calls _tryDecimal with allowTrailingDot parameter to allow '1.' without error when the number doesn't start with +/- or dot. Port _rdNumber (lines 4134-4188) simply checks if next char after dot is a digit, but does not replicate the allowTrailingDot=false error path for standalone dots.

SerializeVisitor does not implement ValueVisitor or SelectorVisitor interfaces. Dart original (line 114-115) implements CssVisitor, ValueVisitor, AND SelectorVisitor. Scala (line 49) only implements CssVisitor[Unit]. Value serialization is done via private formatValue/formatColorDispatch/formatList etc that return String instead of writing to buffer. Selector serialization is done via formatSelectorList/writeComplexSelectorTo/writeCompoundSelectorTo that also return String. This architectural mismatch means value.accept(serializer) and selector.accept(serializer) do not work. Related to ISS-552.

DROPPED: _rdNumber missing --unit disallow. Dart-sass _number (lines 2706-2708) explicitly disallows units beginning with -- (double dash). Port _rdNumber (lines 4185-4186) calls identifier(unit=true) but does not check for leading --.

_performInterpolation missing InterpolationMap support: Dart has _performInterpolationWithMap (line 4388-4398) and _performInterpolationHelper (line 4402-4455) which track targetOffsets for source mapping. Scala has only a simple _performInterpolation that returns String without any InterpolationMap. This blocks source-map generation and span-based error reporting for selectors and media queries.

SerializeVisitor OutputStyle is String-based instead of enum. Dart OutputStyle (lines 1828-1847) is an enum with expanded/compressed variants. Scala OutputStyle (lines 36-39) is an object with String constants. Also missing: Dart LineFeed enum (lines 1849-1872) with cr/crlf/lf/lfcr variants and text property. Scala has no LineFeed type at all. SerializeResult in Dart (lines 1874-1882) is a record with (String css, SingleMapping? sourceMap). Scala SerializeResult (line 42) uses Nullable[String] for sourceMap instead of a proper source map object.

SassCalculation.hashCode uses arguments.hashCode() which relies on Scala List structural equality. Dart calculation.dart:973 uses listHash(arguments) which is a custom hash combiner. Since arguments contain mixed types (SassNumber, CalculationOperation, SassString), the hash behavior may differ when comparing calculations containing CalculationOperation instances.

visitContentBlock implementation differs: Dart (line 1345-1347) throws UnsupportedError('Evaluation handles @include and its content block together'). Scala (line 4333-4341) evaluates the block's children directly as a defensive fallback. This behavioral difference means improperly reached content blocks produce output instead of failing.

SassCalculation.roundInternal missing span parameter: Dart calculation.dart:530 roundInternal takes a required FileSpan? span parameter. Port line 515-521 omits this entirely. The span is not currently used in Dart but is part of the API contract.

SerializeVisitor isNodeInvisible (lines 77-100) does not check inspect flag. Dart _isInvisible (line 1823-1825) returns false when _inspect is true, meaning ALL nodes are visible in inspect mode. Scala isNodeInvisible has no such guard, so invisible nodes (placeholder selectors, hidden comments) would be filtered even in inspect mode, producing incorrect output for meta.inspect().

_runUserDefinedFunction missing @return error: Dart _runFunctionCallable (line 3618-3627) throws 'Function finished without @return' when the function body falls through. Scala _runUserDefinedFunction (line 4603-4630) returns SassNull instead of throwing, silently producing wrong output.

DROPPED: _rdIdentifierLike missing LegacyIfExpression deprecation warning. Dart-sass identifierLike (lines 2958-2974) when parsing legacy if() emits Deprecation.ifFunction warning with a modernSuggestion. Port _rdIdentifierLike (lines 5149-5163) does not emit any deprecation warning for legacy if().

SerializeVisitor isNodeInvisible (lines 77-100) does not use isInvisibleHidingComments for compressed mode. Dart _isInvisible (line 1824-1825) uses node.isInvisibleHidingComments when compressed, node.isInvisible when expanded. Scala reimplements visibility with pattern matching that checks CssComment.isPreserved manually but misses the distinction between isInvisible and isInvisibleHidingComments on other node types like CssStyleRule.
unifyUniversalAndElement missing name==star branch: Dart line 185 has name1==null or name1==star but Scala line 145 only has name1.isEmpty

DROPPED: _rdTrySpecialFunction missing vendored expression() deprecation warning. Dart-sass trySpecialFunction (lines 3337-3378) for vendored expression() checks if argument is invalid or non-CSS SassScript and emits Deprecation.functionName warning with a suggestion. Port _rdTrySpecialFunction (lines 5265-5272) just parses expression() without any deprecation check.

DROPPED: _rdTrySpecialFunction missing vendored progid deprecation warning. Dart-sass trySpecialFunction (lines 3403-3420) emits Deprecation.functionName for vendored progid: prefix. Port _rdTrySpecialFunction (lines 5283-5297) parses progid: without deprecation.

SerializeVisitor isTrailingComment (lines 290-311) has two deviations from Dart _isTrailingComment (lines 1733-1761): (1) Added guard previous.isInstanceOf[CssComment] returning false -- not in the Dart original which allows comment-after-comment trailing. (2) When previous.span.contains(node.span) is true, Dart does lastIndexOf left-brace search to find the opening brace line, while Scala uses simplified nodeSpan.start.line == prevSpan.start.line heuristic. The Dart brace-search accounts for nested blocks where the brace is on a different line than the span start.

colorInSpace helper always passes legacyMissing=false (line 1317), but Dart _colorInSpace defaults legacyMissing=true (line 1627). Affects updateComponents: when color.change/adjust/scale has explicit space, Scala incorrectly fills missing channels with 0 for legacy spaces. Fix: add legacyMissing param defaulting to true, pass false only from toSpaceFn.

SerializeVisitor writeChildrenIn (lines 315-353) does not use _withoutIndentation for trailing comments. Dart _visitChildren (line 1696) calls _withoutIndentation(() => child.accept(this)) to temporarily zero indentation when emitting trailing comments. Scala line 326 just calls child.accept(this) with indentation unchanged. If the trailing comment is a multi-line comment that internally calls writeIndent, the Scala version would emit extra indentation.

DROPPED: _rdTrySpecialFunction missing vendored url() deprecation. Dart-sass _tryUrlContents (lines 3496-3514) emits Deprecation.functionName for vendored url() with invalid SassScript arguments. Port _rdTryUrlContents (lines 5595-5639) does not check vendored flag or emit deprecation.

angleValue (line 1621-1623) missing deprecation warning for non-angle units: Dart _angleValue (line 1459-1467) calls warnForDeprecation when the number has a non-angle unit (not compatible with deg). Scala just returns number.value without warning. This affects adjust-hue and doAdjustChannel for HSL/HWB hue.

DROPPED: _rdArgumentInvocation missing misplaced-rest deprecation warnings. Dart-sass _argumentInvocation (lines 1889-1927) emits Deprecation.misplacedRest when named args follow rest args or positional args follow rest args. Port _rdArgumentInvocation (lines 5832-5908) does not track or warn about misplaced rest arguments.

doAdjustChannel missing checkPercent deprecation for HSL saturation/lightness (line 1596-1598): Dart _adjustChannel line 1275 calls _checkPercent(adjustmentArg, channel.name) before forcing %. Scala skips the deprecation warning and just forces % unit directly.

SerializeVisitor serialize method (lines 153-171) has incorrect charset guard. Dart serialize (line 70) checks charset param AND css.codeUnits.any(>0x7F). Scala serialize (line 165) checks containsNonAscii AND !css.startsWith(@charset) -- the startsWith guard is invented logic not in the Dart. Also missing: the charset parameter entirely (Dart line 55 has charset: true default). The Scala method is also an instance method on the visitor rather than a top-level function.

doAdjustChannel missing deprecation warning for alpha with units (line 1599-1601): Dart _adjustChannel lines 1278-1292 emit warnForDeprecation about passing a number with unit to alpha before stripping units. Scala just strips units silently with SassNumber(adjustmentNum.value).

ARCH: _withStyleRuleChildren not ported. Dart-sass _withStyleRuleChildren (lines 559-580) wraps style rule child parsing with an indented-syntax warning when children is empty (selector doesnt have any properties and wont be rendered). Port _styleRule (lines 1415-1446) sets _inStyleRule directly but misses the empty-children warning.

doChangeColor missing deprecation warning for alpha with non-% units (line 1480): Dart _changeColor lines 1106-1118 emit warnForDeprecation for alpha numbers with units other than %. Scala just calls valueInRange without warning.

SerializeVisitor visitCssKeyframeBlock (line 1834-1838) hardcodes separator as literal comma-space. Dart visitCssKeyframeBlock (lines 296-305) uses _commaSeparator which is comma-space in expanded and bare comma in compressed mode. Scala uses mkString with hardcoded comma-space so compressed keyframe blocks will have unnecessary spaces.

sameFn (line 1838-1855) always converts both colors to xyz-d65 and compares. Dart same (line 714-752) first checks if both are in the same space and compares channels directly, only converting through xyz-d65 when spaces differ. The missing same-space fast path means Scala may report colors as different due to floating-point drift from unnecessary xyz-d65 round-trip.

SerializeVisitor writeImportUrl (lines 1813-1832) uses naive quote wrapping for unquoted URLs in compressed mode. Dart _writeImportUrl (line 292) calls _visitQuotedString(urlContents) which properly escapes backslashes, control characters, and PUA characters. Scala just wraps in double quotes without any escaping, so URLs containing special characters would produce invalid CSS.

ARCH: _propertyOrVariableDeclaration not faithfully ported. Dart-sass version (lines 590-629) handles property hacks, calls _variableDeclarationOrInterpolation for non-plainCss, has nested property check (names beginning with -- may not be nested), and calls _tryDeclarationChildren twice (before and after value). Port _propertyOrVariableDeclaration (lines 2009-2015) only checks for dollar sign and falls through to _declarationOrStyleRule, missing property hack handling and nested -- error.

DROPPED: _configuration missing with-private deprecation. Dart-sass _configuration (lines 1685-1692) emits Deprecation.withPrivate when a configured variable name starts with dash. Port _useRule/_forwardRule configuration parsing (lines 1192-1218, 1276-1314) does not check for or warn about private variable names in with clause.

SerializeVisitor visitCssImport (lines 1796-1806) applies CssMediaQuery.normalizeMediaFeatures to modifiers. Dart visitCssImport (lines 261-274) writes modifiers as raw string via _buffer.write(modifiers) with no normalization. The normalizeMediaFeatures call is invented logic not in the Dart original that may alter modifier output.

DROPPED: _configuration missing duplicate variable check. Dart-sass _configuration (lines 1712-1714) errors when the same variable is configured twice. Port _useRule configuration (lines 1185-1218) uses a ListBuffer without checking for duplicate variable names.

SerializeVisitor _visitMediaQuery (lines 1755-1778) applies CssMediaQuery.normalizeCondition to conditions. Dart _visitMediaQuery (lines 307-329) writes conditions directly via _buffer.write without normalization. The normalizeCondition call is invented logic not in the Dart original. Conditions should be stored pre-normalized or written verbatim.

SerializeVisitor writeFoldedCustomPropertyValue (lines 1540-1557) missing form-feed (0x0C) in whitespace check. Dart _writeFoldedValue (lines 402-417) uses scanner.peekChar().isWhitespace which includes space, tab, LF, CR, AND form-feed (FF). Scala checks space, tab, newline, CR but not form-feed. FF in custom property values after a newline would not be collapsed.

ARCH: @function body uses _children instead of _functionChildren/_withChildren(_functionChild). Dart-sass _functionRule (lines 1000-1011) uses _withChildren(_functionChild,...) which routes through the _functionChild dispatcher (lines 755-795). This dispatcher validates that only variable declarations, @debug/@error/@warn/@each/@for/@if/@return/@while are allowed - style rules and declarations produce helpful errors. Port @function case (lines 375-424) uses generic _children() which allows any statement.

ARCH: @mixin body uses _children instead of _withChildren(_statement). Dart-sass _mixinRule (lines 1490-1502) uses _withChildren(_statement,...) and sets _inMixin inside the callback pattern. Port _mixinRule (lines 2102-2133) sets _inMixin=true before _children() and resets after, but does not use the _withChildren pattern which handles span computation and whitespace differently.

colorFromChannelsModern HSL case (line 353-359) missing checkPercent deprecation for saturation and lightness: Dart _colorFromChannels lines 1854-1855 calls _checkPercent for saturation and lightness before forcePercent. Scala uses forcePercent directly without deprecation warning for unitless numbers.

SerializeVisitor calculation serialization is split between SassCalculation.argumentToCss and SerializeVisitor formatNonFiniteNumber/formatComplexUnitNumber. Dart visitCalculation (lines 520-526) and _writeCalculationValue (lines 527-576) are visitor methods that use _isCompressed to control operator whitespace (line 560: operatorWhitespace = !_isCompressed || operator.precedence == 1). SassCalculation.argumentToCss always uses spaces around operators regardless of compressed mode. _writeCalculationUnits (lines 580-599) uses _writeOptionalSpace which respects compressed mode. SassCalculation.appendCalculationUnits always uses spaces. Both miss compressed-mode optimization.

DROPPED: _rdInterpolatedDeclarationValue (lines 5646-5748) does not handle endAfterOf or consumeNewlines parameters. It also does not handle allowOpenBrace. The method is a partial duplicate of _interpolatedDeclarationValue (lines 4967-5098) which does handle all parameters. Two separate implementations of the same dart-sass method exist in the port.

SerializeVisitor visitCssStylesheet (lines 1316-1346) emits trailing newline unconditionally in non-compressed mode (line 1344: buffer.append newline). Dart visitCssStylesheet (line 191-193) only emits trailing semicolon when previous != null AND requiresSemicolon AND not compressed. The trailing newline itself is correct. However, Scala line 1343-1344 emits both semicolon AND newline in the same if block, while Dart separates them: semicolon on line 192, then no separate trailing newline. Dart ends the stylesheet with just the semicolon (no trailing LF except via the line feed between siblings).

ARCH: _rdInterpolatedDeclarationValue (lines 5646-5748) is a second copy of _interpolatedDeclarationValue (lines 4967-5098) with fewer features. The dart-sass original has ONE method _interpolatedDeclarationValue with 7 parameters. Port has two versions: the full one used by almostAnyValue/supports paths, and a stripped version used by the if-expression and trySpecialFunction paths. These should be unified.

SerializeVisitor formatMap (lines 1064-1081) uses isCompressed for separator but Dart visitMap (lines 1080-1091) always uses comma-space (literal string) and colon-space (literal). Dart map serialization is inspect-only (throws SassScriptException if not inspect) and never uses compressed separators. Scala formatMap uses compressed separators which is incorrect since maps can only be serialized in inspect mode. Also Scala formatMap formatEntryPart checks inspect AND separator comma/slash but Dart _writeMapElement only checks comma separator.

STUB: Text-based _parseSimpleExpression (lines 2413-2629, ~215 LOC) is a regex-like string-splitting expression parser that should be removed. It is only used by _parseParameterList for default values and _parseArgumentList for argument values. These callers should use _rdExpression/expressionUntilComma directly from the scanner, not pre-collect text and then re-parse. Text-based parser mishandles operator precedence, nested function calls, and complex expressions.

SerializeVisitor formatValue (lines 365-376) missing SassScriptException throws for non-serializable values. Dart visitFunction (lines 993-1001) throws SassScriptException when not inspect. Dart visitMixin (lines 1003-1011) throws SassScriptException when not inspect. Dart visitList (lines 1017-1019) throws when empty unbracket list and not inspect. Scala formatValue line 375 falls through to v.toCssString() for functions/mixins without throwing. Missing the guard logic for SassFunction and SassMixin.

ARCH: _parseParameterList (lines 916-1024) pre-collects default value text into StringBuilder then calls _parseSimpleExpression. Dart-sass _parameterList (lines 1804-1849) calls expressionUntilComma() directly from scanner position. Port should use expressionUntilComma() instead of text collection.

ARCH: _parseArgumentList (lines 1029-1136) pre-collects argument text into StringBuilder then calls _parseSimpleExpression. Dart-sass _argumentInvocation (lines 1862-1953) calls expressionUntilComma() directly. Port should use _rdArgumentInvocation instead.

SerializeVisitor formatValue (lines 365-376) missing visitBoolean handling. Dart visitBoolean (line 518) writes value.value.toString(). Scala has no explicit SassBoolean case in formatValue match -- it falls through to the catch-all v.toCssString(). This may work if SassBoolean.toCssString returns the right thing, but the architecture differs from the visitor pattern.

SerializeVisitor formatValue missing SassFunction/SassMixin inspect rendering. Dart visitFunction (lines 993-1001) in inspect mode writes get-function(name) with quoted name via _visitQuotedString. Dart visitMixin (lines 1003-1011) in inspect mode writes get-mixin(name) with quoted name. Scala formatValue has no special cases for SassFunction or SassMixin -- they fall through to v.toCssString() which may not produce get-function(quoted-name) format.

MISSING: _parseSingleProduction not ported (dart-sass line 168-174). Used by parseParameterList, parseExpression, parseNumber, parseVariableDeclaration, parseUseRule. Port inlines the wrapSpanFormatException+expectDone pattern in each method.
MISSING: parseParameterList public entry point not ported (dart-sass lines 126-135). Port has _parseParameterList as private method but no public parseParameterList that scans @rule name first.

DROPPED: _parameterList missing duplicate parameter check. Dart-sass _parameterList (lines 1836-1838) uses a Set named to detect duplicate parameter names and errors. Port _parseParameterList has no duplicate detection.

SerializeVisitor formatList (lines 976-1042) missing empty unbracket list error. Dart visitList (lines 1017-1019) throws SassScriptException for empty unbracket list when not inspect. Scala line 991 returns empty string instead of throwing. Also: Dart singleton handling (line 1048) writes separator.separator then optional closing paren. Scala line 1033-1036 only handles comma and slash separators, missing other separator types.

SerializeVisitor visitCssDeclaration (lines 1492-1527) missing error conversion try/catch. Dart visitCssDeclaration (lines 377-399) wraps value serialization in try/catch for MultiSpanSassScriptException (converting to MultiSpanSassException with span info) and SassScriptException (converting to SassException with span info). Scala has no error handling at all -- exceptions propagate without span enrichment. Related to ISS-748.

SerializeVisitor visitCssDeclaration (lines 1492-1527) emits isImportant with space before exclamation. Dart visitCssDeclaration does NOT handle isImportant -- the important flag is part of the value AST node and serialized through the value visitor. Scala adds explicit isImportant handling (line 1524-1526) which is invented logic that may double-emit important if the value already contains it.

SerializeVisitor writeLegacyColor (lines 472-570) has invented hasNaNChannel guard not in Dart. Dart _writeLegacyColor (line 748) checks !color.isInGamut && !_inspect and writes HSL unconditionally. Scala line 478 adds && !hasNaNChannel(color) which skips HSL for NaN channels. This guard is not in the Dart original and changes behavior for colors with NaN channel values.

SerializeVisitor writeRgb (lines 618-634) uses writeChannel for alpha instead of writeNumber. Dart _writeRgb (lines 885-901) calls _writeNumber(color.alpha) for alpha (line 898). Scala line 630 calls writeChannel(Nullable(color.alpha)) which wraps in Nullable and goes through the none/finite/non-finite dispatch. For alpha this introduces unnecessary overhead and could produce none for missing alpha, but alpha is never missing in _writeRgb since the method is only called when alpha is present.

SerializeVisitor visitCssComment (lines 1684-1712) sourceURL/sourceMappingURL filtering uses case-insensitive startsWith. Dart visitCssComment (line 202) uses case-sensitive regex /\*# source(Mapping)?URL=. Scala line 1689-1691 converts to lowercase before checking. The case-insensitive match could incorrectly filter comments like /*# SourceMappingURL= that the Dart would preserve.

SerializeVisitor visitCssComment (lines 1684-1712) missing minimumIndentation == -1 case. Dart visitCssComment (lines 196-218) has three cases: null (no newlines, write verbatim), -1 (has newlines but no indented lines), and int (has indented lines, write with indent). Scala line 1703 checks minIndent != IntMaxValue AND minIndent >= 0, so the -1 case falls through to the else branch which writes verbatim. Dart writes with minimumIndentation for -1 case too (assert minimumIndentation != -1 on line 205 suggests -1 is an error there, but the actual handling emits writeIndentation + writeWithIndent). Need to verify exact handling.

SerializeVisitor visitQuotedString (lines 872-908) pre-scans for both quote types but does not handle the interleaved restart case. Dart _visitQuotedString (lines 1360-1449) incrementally discovers quotes during emission and restarts with forceDoubleQuote=true when it encounters double-quote after already seeing single-quotes (lines 1372-1374 and 1384-1386). Scala does a full pre-scan then picks one quote char. This produces correct output for strings with only one quote type but may pick the wrong quote for strings containing both types in certain orderings. Related to ISS-747.

SerializeVisitor visitQuotedString (lines 872-908) missing tab literal passthrough. Dart _visitQuotedString (lines 1393-1424) explicitly lists all control characters that get hex-escaped: nul through us and del, but NOT tab (0x09). Scala line 891 checks c < 0x20 && c != tab which correctly excludes tab, BUT also excludes other chars. The Dart uses an exhaustive list of charcode constants. Verify that the Scala condition c < 0x20 matches the same set -- it does exclude LF (0x0A) which is in the Dart list. Actually the Scala handles backslash on line 889 and quote on line 890, and the Dart handles those separately too. Main concern: Scala groups all c < 0x20 except tab into one escape branch, while Dart lists them individually. The net effect should be equivalent since all 0x00-0x1F except 0x09 are in the Dart list.

SerializeVisitor formatColorDispatch (lines 401-438) missing non-legacy color space visit case for hsl/hwb with missing channels using deg unit. Dart visitColor (line 639) passes _isCompressed ? null : 'deg' for channel0 unit. Scala line 425 uses Nullable.Null for compressed. This is correct. However, the Dart case for rgb/hsl/hwb with missing channels (lines 625-645) calls _writeChannel which writes none and the unit. The Scala line 425 passes the deg unit via Nullable which means compressed mode gets Nullable.Null for deg unit. Verify this matches -- it appears to be correct.

SerializeVisitor writeLabLchFunction (lines 639-671) uses hardcoded 100 for lightness max check in relative color syntax guard. Dart (lines 693-694) checks fuzzyInRange(value.channel0, 0, 100) for lab/lch and fuzzyInRange(value.channel0, 0, 1) for oklab/oklch. The lightness max is determined by the isOk flag in formatLabLchColor (line 446) but the lightness check in writeLabLchFunction line 649 uses hardcoded 100 instead of the space-specific max. For oklab/oklch this is wrong -- should use 1, not 100.

SerializeVisitor companion object missing serializeSelector entry point. Dart has top-level function serializeSelector(Selector, inspect: false) at lines 107-111 that creates a visitor and calls selector.accept(visitor). Scala companion object (lines 1841-1966) has serialize and serializeValue but no serializeSelector. The formatSelectorList method exists as a private instance method but is not exposed publicly.

SerializeVisitor.serializeValue (line 1862) missing quote parameter. Dart serializeValue (line 91) has quote: bool = true parameter that is passed to the visitor constructor as _quote field. When quote=false, quoted strings are emitted without quotes. Scala serializeValue has no quote parameter and the visitor constructor has no quote field, so there is no way to suppress quotes. This affects unquoted string interpolation in CSS output.

SerializeVisitor uses StringBuilder instead of SourceMapBuffer. Dart _SerializeVisitor (line 117-118) uses SourceMapBuffer (when sourceMap=true) or NoSourceMapBuffer. The _for method (line 1675) and _buffer.forSpan calls throughout provide per-token source map entries. Scala uses plain StringBuilder (line 51) with a manual recordMapping helper. The Dart approach provides precise per-token mappings via forSpan wrapping in visitCssAtRule, visitCssImport, visitCssDeclaration, visitCssComment, visitCssStyleRule, visitCssMediaRule, visitCssSupportsRule, and visitCssKeyframeBlock. Scala only calls recordMapping in visitCssStyleRule and visitCssDeclaration. Related to ISS-355.

SerializeVisitor writeCompoundSelectorTo (lines 1448-1490) relies on simple.toString for non-pseudo/non-placeholder selectors instead of implementing proper visitor methods. Dart visitAttributeSelector (lines 1532-1551) has complex logic: checks Parser.isIdentifier for value, avoids quoting --prefixed values for IE11 compat, uses _visitQuotedString for non-identifier values with proper escaping, handles modifier spacing differently for identifier vs quoted values. Scala delegates to AttributeSelector.toString which may not match this formatting. Also Dart visitUniversalSelector (lines 1663-1669) handles namespace prefix with pipe. The selector toString methods would need to exactly replicate the Dart visitor formatting.

SerializeVisitor writeCompoundSelectorTo pseudo handling (lines 1452-1479) does not filter inner selector list through the serializer's own selector formatting. Dart visitPseudoSelector (line 1655) calls pseudo.selector.andThen(visitSelectorList) which uses the serializer's visitSelectorList (with lineBreak/compressed handling). Scala line 1472-1476 manually iterates visible complexes and calls writeComplexSelectorTo with hardcoded comma-space separator. This misses: (1) lineBreak handling from visitSelectorList, (2) compressed-mode comma-only separators, (3) the Dart uses _writeOptionalSpace for non-linebreak selectors.

SerializeVisitor writeReindentedCustomPropertyValue (line 1567) uses trimRight which trims all trailing whitespace. Dart _writeReindentedValue (line 427) uses trimAsciiRight(value, excludeEscape: true) which preserves trailing backslash-escape sequences. If a custom property value ends with a CSS escape like backslash-space, the Scala version would incorrectly trim it.

SerializeVisitor visitCssStylesheet (line 1344) adds trailing newline after the last child that the Dart original does not emit. Dart visitCssStylesheet (lines 191-193) only emits a semicolon after the last child if needed, with no trailing newline. The Scala appends buffer.append(newline) unconditionally in expanded mode. This causes the serialized CSS to end with an extra newline character not present in dart-sass output. Note: this may be intentional as many CSS serializers add a trailing newline, but it deviates from the Dart source.

SerializeVisitor formatSelectorList (lines 1365-1398) missing inspect-mode handling. Dart visitSelectorList (line 1604) includes ALL components when _inspect is true, including invisible ones. Scala formatSelectorList (line 1370) always filters with filterNot(_.isInvisible) regardless of inspect flag. In inspect mode, invisible selectors (containing placeholders) should be included in the output.

SerializeVisitor formatSelectorList (line 1371) returns empty string for fully invisible selector lists. Dart visitSelectorList (lines 1603-1622) would simply write nothing to the buffer (no special return). Since Scala uses string return, the caller appends an empty string which is benign but architecturally different. The Dart approach would leave the buffer position unchanged while the Scala appends empty string to buffer.
